title: T-Echo boots wedged after DFU reflash: SMP alive, console and radio dead, survives reboots
description: After today's reflash cycle (1200-bps touch -> serial DFU, one SIGPIPE-aborted attempt, then a clean 'Device programmed', then UF2 rewrite of the identical combined image), the T-Echo comes up in a consistent wedged state on every boot: USB enumerates all three CDC interfaces (~100-110 s), SMP on if04 answers (echo + image state: slot0 v1.0.0 active/confirmed/bootable, hash aba65119d91f8dcc...), but the Zephyr console on if02 emits zero bytes and the radio never transmits (gateway heard nothing in 3x 70 s windows, on BOTH pre-merge and post-merge gateway builds; T-Echo pings every 15 s when healthy). Flash content verified byte-perfect against the intended combined image via CURRENT.UF2 readback, and the app binary is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the image that ran fine for 2 h this morning (reproducible build). Erasing the partial slot1 garbage over SMP + reboot did not help. SMP reset + reboot did not help. Distinct from ibk5 (console stall with radio+SMP fine) and 1jqj (USB -110 wedge). Suspect peripheral/power-domain state that only a full power removal clears. NEXT: physical power cycle, then recapture if02 console for the boot banner (radio init errors were invisible this whole time).
notes: 2026-07-15 exhaustive remote debug session, all firmware-reachable levers exhausted. ELIMINATED: (1) firmware content - flashed image byte-identical to the morning-healthy Jul-10 build (reproducible builds, md5 cbd7285b); (2) flash corruption - verified via CURRENT.UF2 readback; (3) slot1 partial-OTA garbage - erased over SMP; (4) peripheral rail latch-up - one-shot railcycle app drove P0.12 low 12 s (SX1262+GPS+eink+sensor genuinely unpowered), no change; (5) nRF sticky state short of POR - System OFF entered with GPIO-sense wake, user button woke it, wedge persists; (6) console-blocking-main theory - console-less build (LOG/PRINTK/CONSOLE all =n) still radio-silent; (7) boot race - 10+ consecutive wedged boots; (8) gateway regression - exact Jul-10 gateway binary (md5 0a44405c) hears nothing either. NEW SYMPTOM: CDC transports degrade over time within a boot - SMP if04 answered early (echo, image state, erase, reset) then began hanging on open/read, same pattern as the earlier pre-reflash SMP wedge and ibk5 console stalls. Device currently runs a diagnostic build (v1.0.0+2) with MCUMGR_GRP_OS_TASKSTAT + THREAD_MONITOR + THREAD_NAME enabled - first taskstat reply came back with an empty tasks map, retries hung. NEXT: (a) true VDD removal - open case, disconnect battery JST (only power state deeper than System OFF); (b) PPK2 current profiling to see whether TX bursts happen at all; (c) on any healthy boot, SMP taskstat is now available on-device. Tooling built this session (scratchpad railcycle/ + UF2 flash flow via 1200-touch + udisksctl, no buttons needed) makes iteration cheap.
close_reason: RESOLVED by true VDD removal. User disconnected the battery (case open) and replugged; board came back in UF2 bootloader mode (railcycle one-shot second-boot path). Reflashed MCUboot+puck from the post-merge tree (combined UF2 via mass-storage copy, md5 14d34091). Board now fully healthy: all 3 CDC up in seconds (vs 100-110s wedged), console streaming on if02, SX1262 RX-cycling, CoAP GET /status TX every 15s at uptime 6min. Confirms diagnosis: peripheral/power-domain state only a full power removal clears - System OFF was NOT deep enough, battery JST disconnect was. Every firmware-reachable lever had been exhausted (see notes); if it recurs, go straight to battery pop.
